chapters.narration: Some paths are redundant. If the river path and the perpendicular path already reach everywhere, a diagonal path adds nothing new. Vectors are linearly independent when none can be built from the others. Each one opens a genuinely new direction. The test: c one u plus c two v equals zero only when all coefficients are zero.

In two dimensions you can have at most two independent vectors. In three dimensions, at most three. Add more and someone becomes a passenger. Geometrically, dependent vectors are collinear. Independent vectors span the full space — no wasted directions.
